'''Clint Greenshields''' (born 11 January 1982) is a former [[France national rugby league team|France]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|fb}} for the [[St George Illawarra Dragons]] and the [[North Queensland Cowboys]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]], and for the [[Catalans Dragons]] in the [[Super League]].

==Early years==
{{BLP unreferenced section|date=September 2023}}
Greenshields was born in [[Coffs Harbour, New South Wales]], Australia.

A former [[Sawtell, New South Wales|Sawtell]] resident, Greenshields attended Toormina High School, who compete every year in the Robbie MacKinnon Cup against other local schools of the Coffs Harbour district. His junior club was the Sawtell Panthers JRLFC, a club recently crowned Group 2 senior champions.

==Playing career==
Greenshields made his first grade debut for the [[St George Illawarra Dragons]] after spending time in the lower grades for the [[Brisbane Broncos]] and [[Wests Tigers]].<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.coffscoastadvocate.com.au/news/greenshields-makes-nrl-return/1557346/|title=Greenshields to make NRL return|newspaper=Daily Telegraph|date=25 September 2012|last1=Houghton|first1=By Nick}}</ref>

He moved to the [[Catalans Dragons]] in 2007 and took the role as first-choice full back, becoming a key player. On 10 May 2008 it was announced that Greenshields has signed a new three-year contract with the French Super League club.<ref name="Greenshields signs Catalans deal">{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/catalan/7393730.stm|title=Greenshields signs Catalans deal|publisher=Quins|date=2008-05-10|access-date=2008-05-10}}</ref>

Greenshields was named in the 2008 [[Super League Dream Team]], beating world class talents like [[Brent Webb]] and [[Paul Wellens]] to be named as the outstanding full back of the Super League season. He was also one of the front runners for the [[Man of Steel Awards|Man of Steel award]] in 2008.<ref name="2008 engage Super League Dream Team">{{cite web|url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/article.php?id=11755|title=2008 engage Super League Dream Team|publisher=Super League|date=2008-09-15|accessdate=2008-09-26|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20080918211731/http://www.superleague.co.uk/article.php?id=11755|archivedate=18 September 2008}}</ref>

Greenshields signed a one-year extension to his deal at Catalans Dragons in July 2011.<ref>[http://www.superleague.co.uk/article.php?id=22813 6 July 2011.] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120910174021/http://www.superleague.co.uk/article.php?id=22813 |date=10 September 2012 }}</ref>

Greenshields returned to the NRL in 2013, signing with the [[North Queensland Cowboys]], for whom he played 6 times, scoring three tries. He subsequently declared that the 2013 NRL season would be his last as a professional rugby league player, and announced his retirement at the end of the year.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.stuff.co.nz/sport/league/9010315/Injury-ensures-retirement-for-another-Cowboy|title = Injury ensures retirement for another Cowboy|date = 6 August 2013}}</ref> However, he later changed his mind and went on to win two Group 2 grand finals, 2017 and 2019 with the Grafton Ghosts.

===Representative career===
Qualifying via residency, Greenshields was selected for the [[France national rugby league team|French national rugby league team]] to compete in the [[2009 Rugby League Four Nations|2009 Four Nations tournament]].<ref>{{cite web |title=France include three Catalans Australians for Four Nations |date=2009-09-30 |website=[[The Guardian]] |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190402075201/https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2009/sep/30/catalans-australians-france-four-nations |archive-date=2019-04-02 |url-status=live |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2009/sep/30/catalans-australians-france-four-nations}}</ref>
He made his debut against England on 23 October 2009, and played in the match against [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.nrl.com/newsviews/latestnews/newsarticle/tabid/10874/newsid/57245/greenshields-dares-to-live-french-dream/default.aspx |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20091109050532/http://www.nrl.com/newsviews/latestnews/newsarticle/tabid/10874/newsid/57245/greenshields-dares-to-live-french-dream/default.aspx |archive-date=9 November 2009 |title=Greenshields dares to live French dream – NRL.com}}</ref>

He was named in the France squad for the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref>[https://archive.today/20130924162628/http://www.ffr13.fr/index.php/equipes-nationales/equipe-de-france/index.php?option=com_content&view=article&id=5901]</ref>

== Career highlights ==
*''Junior Club:'' Sawtell Panthers JRLFC
*''First Grade Debut:'' Round 9, St George Illawarra v Manly, Brookvale Oval, 9 May 2004
